explorers.summary: The legacy per-Entity sync (~23ms 'rend' at 24 heroes/80 enemies/24 buildings) splits into three cost classes I measured directly. (1) Pure no-change Python/Panda property overhead: every visible unit re-runs sync_hp_bar (25.2us), ensure_ks_name_label (16.0us), sync_hero_gold_label (15.2us) and sync_hero_overlays_facing (44.7us hero / ~28us enemy) EVERY frame even when nothing changed, because Ursina's enabled-setter is unconditional (unstash + loose_children walk = 9.25us per redundant 'enabled=True') and sync_ks_facing_overlay writes scale_x+rotation_y unconditionally (9.37us per child) — at ~73 in-view units that is ~6-7ms/frame of fully skippable work. (2) Per-frame filesystem stats: _resolve_prefab_path does Path.is_file() per building per frame, measured 87us/call on this OneDrive tree → ~2.1ms at 24 buildings, ~8.7ms at 100, worse during construction (staged resolver stats multiple candidates). (3) Structural node count: ~241 overlay child nodes (HP bg/fg quads + Text labels) are each separate scene-graph nodes/draw calls; Ursina Text creation costs 5.7ms median, which is the hero/enemy-spawn micro-hitch (a wave of 10 enemies entering view = ~57ms hitch frame). build_snapshot adds ~1.3ms/frame of DTO allocation (5.6us/unit, 7.2us/building, rebuilt every render frame). Dirty-gating + caching (S effort) recovers ~8-10ms of legacy rend, but the legacy path cannot reach 30fps alone; the structural winner is a batched/instanced overlay renderer that BOTH cuts legacy node count AND fills the known overlay gap that blocks shipping the 2.5x instanced unit path (its branch returns at ursina_renderer.py:612 before any overlay sync).

The instanced unit path (KINGDOM_URSINA_INSTANCING=1) is the single biggest lever in the codebase: measured 13-15 fps legacy vs 31.5 avg windowed / 25.2 maximized at the gate scenario (wk123 plan results log), with rend collapsing 23ms to ~7ms. It is blocked from default-on by one feature gap and several correctness gaps. The feature gap: ursina_renderer.update() returns at line 612 before any unit-sync call, so HP bars, name labels, hero gold labels, and the tax-collector gold label are never created (measured: 241 overlay nodes legacy vs 0 instanced). The correctness gaps found by line-by-line diff of both paths: instanced units ignore terrain height (flat y=scale*0.5 vs legacy get_terrain_height; TERRAIN_HEIGHT_SCALE=5.0 means units bury into hills), all projectiles render as arrows (WK124 magic/heal kinds missing from the atlas), guards/peasants are squashed to uniform scale (legacy is non-uniform, and ursina_pick assumes legacy heights), the underground layer gate is skipped (dormant feature, low risk), and the added blob shadows pin to y=-0.048 instead of following terrain. Everything else survives: enemy fog gating, inside-building pass, anim FSM/facing parity, picking (pure math, no Entity dependence), bounties/rubble/buildings + their world-space UI (Entity-based in both branches), and the rest-Zzz label is dead code in legacy too. HP bars are trivially instanceable as ONE extra draw call (per-instance pos+fill-fraction, color derived in-shader), and name/gold text is best served by pre-rendered label cards instanced through the same pipeline plus a zoom LOD that culls all labels at the gate scenario's zoomed-out view (owner-authorized; HP bars stay). With those built, flipping the default converts the gate scenario from ~15 fps to ~30-31 windowed (and ~25 maximized, which remains HUD-upload/GPU-fill bound, outside this area), and eliminates hero-spawn micro-hitches because spawn no longer creates Entity+Text nodes. Buildings are a separate, smaller win: prefabs instantiate up to ~26 child Entities each, and per-building flattenStrong (loading pieces as Panda NodePaths instead of Ursina Entities) would cut draw calls and scene.entities walk cost at the 50-100-building pile.

Measured headless on this box at the gate scenario (24 heroes / 80 enemies / ~45-61 buildings / 2083 trees, BasicAI wired): one sim tick costs mean 4.2-6.3 ms, p95 ~6.7 ms, p99 ~10 ms, worst observed 11-35 ms. At FAST (multiplier 1.0 = 20 ticks/wall-second, engine.py:1045, config.py:93-99) the sim burns ~85-120 ms CPU per wall-second, and frames at 13-15 fps bank ~1.5 ticks so they alternate 1- and 2-tick frames (4-12 ms of sim inside a render frame). A/B no-op deltas attribute the cost: fog ~1.45 ms/tick, hero AI ~1.30, hero-profile snapshots rebuilt for every hero every tick ~0.86 (UI-only data built at sim cadence), the 2083-entry tree-growth dict rebuilt every tick ~0.89 (growth is discrete, changing only at 2-minute boundaries), and POI discovery O(POIs x heroes) every tick ~0.59. The worst-tick spikes — the micro-hitch signature, including hero-spawn ticks which force a fog rebuild plus a first A* — are A* bursts (single ticks carrying 6-7.5 ms of pathfinding; the budget permits 24 plans/24k expansions per tick and chase goals key on integer-pixel positions of moving targets so they replan every 500 ms) and fog full rebuilds (1.5-5 ms each, every 3rd tick whenever any of ~50 revealers crosses a tile). A 6000-tick soak shows no cost growth — the WK123 fixes hold; this is steady-state cost. No shared spatial index exists (separation builds a throwaway grid per call; enemy retargeting, combat range checks, and AI threat gates are all linear scans), which is the scaling wall for the owner's hundreds-of-enemies ambition. Candidates 1-7 are largely independent and should cut mean tick cost from ~5 ms to ~1.5-2.5 ms and p99 from 10-35 ms to under ~8 ms; candidate 8 (FAST-speed dt scaling) halves whatever remains, with the renderer's existing sim_blend_fraction interpolation keeping motion smooth.

The HUD path costs ~16-19ms/frame at maximized (hudR 3.9-4.8ms compose + hudU 11.6-14.2ms upload per tmp/wk123/soak_out_fast_maximized.log) vs ~10ms windowed — it is the single largest CPU stage and explains most of the 31-vs-25fps maximized gap. The hudU pipeline (game/graphics/ursina_app_ui_overlay.py:_refresh_ui_overlay_texture) pays, on EVERY changed frame: pygame.image.tobytes full-surface RGBA conversion (measured 7.6ms at 1920x1032 on this exact box), a full numpy row-diff (2.7ms), and per-band uploads through a PandaTexture→PNMImage→load_sub_image round trip (10.7ms per 180-row band, 3.1ms per 48-row band). Worse, the >50%-dirty / >6-run fallback calls setRamImageAs(full) which measures 50.4ms — a guaranteed visible hitch, and hero-spawn produces exactly the scattered multi-region dirty pattern (top-bar Heroes count + message feed + radar dot + left panel) that trips it; wave toasts fade alpha every frame in the same mid-band. The radar (224x180, hud_radar.py) redraws ~190 entity dots per frame, which is what makes the bottom band dirty every single frame and forces the whole changed-frame pipeline. The decisive measured finding: the engine.screen SRCALPHA surface's native memory layout (masks 0xff0000/0xff00/0xff/0xff000000) is byte-identical to Panda3D's F_rgba native BGRA RAM image (verified byte-equal on this box), so the entire convert/swizzle stack can be replaced by direct memoryview copies: band write 0.04ms (250x faster), full fallback ~0.3-7.7ms (7-150x faster), no tobytes at all. Combined with throttling radar dot redraw to 10Hz (making most frames hit the existing 0.57ms fingerprint early-out), hudU drops from ~12ms avg to ~2ms and the 50ms hitch class disappears — worth roughly +6-9 fps at the gate scenario maximized, essentially closing the windowed/maximized gap. A separate radar/topbar quad split is a clean structural follow-up but is no longer required for the gate once the zero-copy path lands. The watch-card pinned-hero minimap (render_coordinator.py) re-renders a full world minimap every frame when pinned and must be throttled or it re-opens the hitch path during real play.

The per-frame sequence is fully serial on one thread: Ursina's _update task computes time.dt, runs mouse.update (2 collision traverses + pointer polls that Kingdom never consumes — picking is analytic in ursina_pick.py), then __main__.update = run_frame (gc-freeze check -> canvas sync -> pointer motion (cached, but invalidated every frame the camera moves) -> tick_simulation (handle_events + 0-4 fixed 50ms sim ticks, each calling a full get_game_state) -> fov/zoom sync -> build_snapshot + renderer.update -> render_pygame (HUD compose, includes 1 more get_game_state via FrameContext) -> HUD texture upload -> pan/terrain-clamp/auto-follow/zone-fog block), then a Python walk over ALL scene.entities (measured 1.30ms at 539 entities, 2.69ms at 1000 on this box), then Panda cull+draw+present with vsync ON (Panda default sync-video=true; Ursina's vsync arg is dead code in this version). The camera/fog/pointer block itself is already well-gated and cheap (zone-fog cached, terrain clamp ~us) — the real "untracked remainder" is OUTSIDE run_frame: soak logs show dt 43-48ms vs run_frame CPU 30-33ms at out/fast/maximized (11-15ms/frame gap, spiking 24-39ms), and at out/fast/windowed dt clusters at 33.9-35.7ms (the 2-vblank 60Hz bucket) against only ~23.5ms of CPU — strong evidence ~10ms/frame is vsync present-wait, which alone explains that combo sitting at 28-29.5fps instead of >35. Separately, get_game_state redundantly builds hero-profile DTOs for all heroes on every sim tick and on every pointer-path call although only HUD code consumes them (~1.5-3ms/frame at fast speed). The spawn micro-hitch is confirmed and quantified: every unit's name label is an Ursina Text whose construction costs 21.0ms (measured; mutation is 0.13ms, plain Panda TextNode 0.41ms), so each hero/enemy spawn (and first gold-label/Zzz) is a 21-80ms spike frame at the swarm. Biggest frame-loop levers in order: kill vsync (S, likely flips the gate combo over 30), pool the Text labels (kills the hitch), lazy hero profiles, trim/bypass the Ursina entity walk (scales with the hundreds-of-enemies goal), and an env-gated Panda Cull/Draw threaded-pipeline experiment to overlap CPU with GPU.

The hero-spawn micro-hitch is almost entirely render-side, and the single dominant cost is Ursina Text label creation: every spawned unit's name/gold label calls ursina.text.Text.__init__, whose font_setter re-runs FontPool.load_font AND appends a duplicate directory to Panda's global model-path on EVERY Text (measured: 5.25-6.04 ms per Text on this box, 73% of it in load_font; model-path grows 16->216 dirs after ~200 Texts, degrading Text creation 5.9->7.3 ms over a session — a previously unknown time-degradation amplifier). Sim-side spawn cost is negligible (Hero.__init__ is plain field init, audio SFX are preloaded, gc thresholds already raised), so the fix surface is graphics-only. A measured property-setter patch (cache the font object, no-op redundant setPixelsPerUnit) cuts Text creation to 0.27 ms and the full hero-spawn render bundle (billboard + 2 HP-bar quads + name Text) from ~6.6 ms to 0.84 ms — a 6-enemy wave entering the zoomed-out viewport currently costs ~35-40 ms in one frame and would drop to ~4 ms. Building construction hitches are real and bigger: the WK122 prewarm caches only mesh parses, while each prefab instantiation (placement, neutral spawn every ~6 s, and each of the 3-4 construction-stage swaps) re-reads/parses the prefab JSON from disk and rebuilds up to ~26 child Entities with per-piece glTF color traversal — measured 2.6-64.4 ms per instantiate (inn_v2 = 64 ms) even after prewarm. Worse, the stage-swap destroy path frees only the prefab root, orphaning all piece children into scene.entities (measured +84 orphans in 5 swap cycles) — a C1-class leak the WK123 fix does not cover, which re-introduces time-degradation in construction-heavy real play and makes every subsequent ursina.destroy()'s linear `entity in scene.entities` scan slower. Finally, several one-time cold loads still hitch the first gameplay frames because the startup prewarm covers only building piece models: the 2048x2048 unit atlas builds lazily on the first unit render (measured 269 ms CPU + ~16 MB GPU upload), the projectile/magic/heal billboard textures generate on the first arrow volley, and the first Text loads the font. Note for the instancing roadmap: the instanced unit path has zero per-spawn node creation (its spawn behavior is ideal), but when C9 restores overlays for instanced units, these same Text costs return unless the font patch and pooling land first.

At the gate scenario (out/fast, ~100+ units, maximized) the WK123 soak telemetry shows dt=42-48ms (~23fps) split as: HUD texture upload (hudU) 11-14ms, sim tick 6.5-8ms, Ursina renderer sync 6.8-7.1ms, pygame HUD compose 4.0ms, and ~13ms GPU/Ursina-internal (tmp/wk123/soak_out_fast_maximized.log). The single largest CPU stage — hudU — is almost entirely allocation/conversion churn: pygame.image.tobytes allocates+swizzles an 8.3MB bytes object every changed frame (measured 8.0ms on this box), the dirty-band path then pays 7.2ms per band in PandaTexture+PNMImage conversions, and the >50%-dirty fallback setRamImageAs("RGBA") costs a measured 52.8ms (matching the 25-69ms hudU spikes across 1303 slowframes). I verified headlessly that the pygame SRCALPHA surface's native byte order and Panda's native FRgba RAM order are both BGRA, so a direct memoryview memcpy (surf.get_view('1') -> tex.modify_ram_image()) is pixel-identical and runs in 0.69ms — a ~10-13ms/frame win that also eliminates ~190MB/s of bytes churn. Second, build_snapshot runs TWICE per frame (Ursina loop + FrameContext inside render_pygame) with no sim tick between, duplicating a measured 1.03ms DTO pass plus bounty-metric recompute. Third, the sim tick rebuilds a 2083-entry tree-growth dict (one fresh key-tuple per tree) every tick — 0.445ms x 2-3 ticks/frame at fast speed, and the dominant gen0 GC feeder (~6k tracked allocs/frame -> a measured 1.9ms gen0 pause every ~7-15 frames = visible jitter). Fourth, the legacy unit path writes .enabled=True and overlay facing properties unconditionally per overlay child per frame; Ursina's enabled-setter has no early-out and calls NodePath.unstash() every time (400 redundant unstash calls measured at 3.5ms). Together these alloc-side fixes are worth roughly 14-18ms/frame at the maximized gate scenario (~23fps -> ~33-36fps) on the default legacy path, plus removal of the 25-69ms HUD-upload hitches and the periodic ~2ms gen0 stalls that read as micro-hitches.

The app boots Ursina 8.3.0 / Panda3D 1.10.16 with zero engine-level tuning: no loadPrcFileData anywhere in game/ (grep), so every Panda default applies. Three big engine-level losses were confirmed. (1) VSYNC IS ON: panda default sync-video=True (measured) and ursina hard-forces window vsync True in apply_settings (window.py:82) — the Ursina(vsync=...) kwarg is silently dropped in 8.3.0 (_ready accepts it but never uses it). The soak logs prove the cost: the per-frame untracked gap (dt minus run_frame CPU) clusters at ~15-16.8ms and ~33-36ms — exact vblank multiples (tmp/wk123/soak_out_fast_maximized.log:59-103). (2) Single-threaded pipeline: threading-model='' (measured), true-threads wheel available; cull+draw+swap sit serialized after ~23ms of Python frame work (instanced gate combo dt 35.7ms vs 23.4ms tracked CPU — soak_out_fast_windowed.log:378). (3) Ursina's per-entity _update loop costs a measured 5.29ms/frame at the legacy scene size (536 entities) and drops to 0.09ms with ignore=True — pure engine overhead since renderer-managed entities have no update/input. Additionally, ursina Text creation measured 5.44ms each, and hero spawn creates 1-2 Texts + 2 quad entities (ursina_unit_overlays.py) — the hero-spawn micro-hitch mechanism. Mouse picking is near-free here (zero world colliders), trees are opaque instanced meshes (no overdraw issue), the 64x64 grass texture cannot thrash texture cache (mipmap candidate ruled OUT by measurement), and development_mode=False is already set. Stacked, the three config fixes are worth roughly 6-12ms/frame at the gate scenario — on the instanced path that is the difference between the borderline 31.5/25.2 fps and a solid >35 windowed / ~30 maximized; on today's legacy default they take ~13-15 to ~17-19 fps (still instancing-gated for 30+).

Prior art is extensive and the dead ends are well documented. TRIED+KEPT (do NOT undo): sim dt-clamp, multi-band HUD dirty upload, gc.freeze+thresholds, stationary-pointer cache, building-prefab prewarm, building pos/color dirty-gates, zone-fog cache, fog-overlay early-out gate (ursina_fog_overlay.py:60-63, was 19ms/frame), cave-shader DCE guard, fog/chunk visibility composition + camLens frustum rect + 8x8 static terrain batching + tree instancing (~2,083 trees -> ~16 draws, CHANGELOG v1.5.8), HUD PIL-bypass + GPU Y-flip + dirty-row upload (v1.5.7: hudU P90 49->12.7ms), unit texture atlas + global pathfinding budget + fixed-rate 20Hz sim tick + linear sim_blend interpolation (v1.6 rounds), and WK123's C1 overlay-destroy fix (ursina_unit_overlays.py:105), C2 dead-unit DTO-skip/peasant-cull/30s hero TTL, C7 set_two_sided on instanced unit geoms, C8 profile cache. TRIED+REJECTED (do NOT retry): GeoMipTerrain LOD (dormant, KINGDOM_URSINA_GEOMIPTERRAIN=0 — no FPS win + elevation distortion); blind sampler/alpha/binding swaps on the instanced shader (5 failed attempts; root cause was backface cull — sampler, UV V-flip and OmniBoundingVolume are verified correct, keep them); instancing default-ON (commit 7fe0828, reverted 0f8ca6d) — now blocked only on overlay parity, not invisibility; exponential constant-alpha lerp ("lurch then crawl"); sync-key early-outs that skip interpolation; headless sim-RSS leak hunting (empirically no sim-side leak); 2-min normal-speed probes as validation; tree/grass density cuts and MAX_ALIVE_ENEMIES reduction (hard-forbidden). KEY MEASURED NUMBERS at the gate spec (24h/24b/80e): legacy default = 13.1 avg fps out/fast/maximized (rend 23.3ms, tick 12.4ms, hudU 14.5ms) and 15.4 best-combo; instanced = rend collapses to ~7ms giving 31.5 avg out/fast/windowed and 25.2 maximized but with ZERO unit overlays (legacy scene=536 entities incl. 241 overlays vs instanced 222/0); 82 instanced enemies alone ~50 fps, +24 heroes costs ~17ms/frame of which only ~6ms is tick+rend — the rest is GPU draw + ~330 overlay nodes + maximized HUD upload. The single biggest documented unbuilt lever is therefore finishing C9 (overlays under instancing) + the now-authorized overlay zoom-LOD and HUD/radar throttle ideas the WK123 plan explicitly deferred to Jaimie's call, plus the never-built building batching lever from the guardrails doc and the deferred WK58 mitigations (numpy fog rebuild, prefab/terrain-height caches).

At the gate scenario (out/fast, ~100+ units, integrated GPU) the default build spends a ~70ms frame roughly as: ~23ms legacy per-Entity unit sync ("rend", ursina_unit_sync.py full resync of every unit + ~240 overlay child nodes), ~7-12ms sim tick run inline on the render thread (2x fast speed doubles tick count), ~4ms pygame HUD compose, ~12-14ms full-window HUD->GPU upload, and ~13ms Panda cull/draw/swap serialized after all the Python work (soak logs: dt 42-47ms has CPU sum ~30ms even on the INSTANCED path). The architecture is a classic retained-OO design: live sim objects -> per-frame frozen DTO tuples (built TWICE per frame — once for the 3D renderer, once again inside render_pygame's FrameContext) -> a per-Entity scene-graph resync. The instanced renderer (opt-in) already proves the render wall is the per-Entity path: flipping it on collapses rend 23ms->7ms and lifts the gate combo from ~13-15 to ~25-31 fps, but it ships dark (it early-returns before overlay creation, so HP bars/names never exist — the documented C9 gap). The highest-leverage redesigns, in order: (1) make instancing the ONLY unit path with instanced HP bars + zoom-LOD pooled name labels and delete the legacy path (gets the measured 2.5x and fixes hero-spawn Text-creation hitches); (2) split the monolithic full-window HUD surface into per-region render targets so the radar/top-bar churn stops forcing an 8MB tobytes + full-frame numpy diff every frame (~10ms back at maximized); (3) move the sim accumulator off the render thread (or at minimum halve fast-speed tick rate), which removes the 2x-speed tax from the frame loop — the DTO/snapshot boundary already in place makes this genuinely buildable; (4) numpy SoA unit storage feeding the instance buffer near-zero-copy, which is the enabler for "hundreds of enemies"; (5) a one-line Panda `threading-model Cull/Draw` experiment to overlap the ~13ms C++ cull/draw with next-frame Python. C1+C2 alone project to ~33fps sustained at the worst combo (maximized+fast+out), and the stack gives comfortable headroom toward hundreds of enemies.

The WK123 measurement stack is solid for its original 17-min leak hunt but has concrete holes for the new minute-15-to-20 gate. Per-frame stage timing exists in run_frame (KINGDOM_FPS_SLOWLOG, tick/rend/hudR/hudU at game/graphics/ursina_app_frame.py:92-196) but is only exported as 120-frame stdout averages; the soak CSV's stage columns are dead — wk123_fps_soak.py:253 reads an engine attr (_wk123_last_stage_ms) that nothing ever sets, and its 'tick' column actually logs the whole frame dt (ursina_app_frame.py:97 vs wk123_fps_soak.py:258). The biggest unattributed cost block is everything outside run_frame (Panda igLoop cull+draw+swap+GPU wait): it is only printed on >40ms slowframes (ursina_app_frame.py:320) and never split — no igLoop/PStats instrumentation exists anywhere in game/. The screenshot path is actively dangerous for a 100-shot gate window: each in-loop shot forces TWO extra renderFrame() calls (~130-150ms at 13-15fps) plus a synchronous 1080p PNG encode I measured at 70-74ms on this box — roughly a 220-290ms hitch every 3s, which would corrupt the very FPS percentiles being gated. There is no spawn-hitch capture mode at all, despite hero-spawn micro-hitches being a named goal (spawn cost lands in rend via per-spawn Entity + Ursina Text label creation, ursina_unit_overlays.py:164-172). On the headless question: perf_render_benchmark.py does NOT isolate renderer.update (it samples whole-loop FPS around /revealmap with a 45-fps terrain bar); perf_stress_test.py is the real renderer.update isolator but opens a live GL window, never ticks the sim (stationary units under-exercise dirty paths), and uses a non-gate scenario. Seven harness-extension candidates below give the sprint per-frame CSV attribution, an igLoop split, cheap async screenshots, spawn-hitch capture, a windowed gate verdict, and an agent-runnable offscreen renderer bench.

synthesis.headline: The lag is four stacked costs, each independently measured: (1) vsync quantization — Panda's default sync-video=true rounds every frame up to a 16.7ms vblank multiple and the Ursina vsync kwarg is dead code, costing ~10-16ms/frame at exactly the 30fps boundary; (2) the legacy per-Entity unit render path (~23ms rend at spec) for which a proven 2.5x instanced path already exists (31.5 fps windowed / 25.2 maximized measured) but is blocked from default by overlay parity gaps; (3) the HUD upload conversion stack (~12-14ms/frame maximized with a measured 50ms full-upload hitch fallback) which a verified byte-identical BGRA memcpy reduces to ~0.7-4ms; (4) sim tick doubled at FAST speed (~6-12ms/frame) dominated by five cacheable per-tick rebuilds. The hero-spawn hitch is root-caused and CONFIRMED: every Ursina Text() re-runs FontPool.load_font and appends a duplicate model-path dir (5.3-21ms per label, degrading over the session), plus prefab construction-stage swaps that cost 11-64ms AND leak ~26 orphan entities per swap (an uncovered C1-class leak that re-introduces time-degradation in real play). Strategy: ship the confirmed S-effort wins first (vsync off, HUD zero-copy, font patch, orphan-free, dirty gates, lazy profiles, caches) — these alone should put windowed past 30 and kill the hitches — then execute the instancing-default stack (instanced HP bars + parity fixes + zoom-LOD labels + linear interp + flip), which is the only mapped route to a solid >30 sustained at the maximized worst combo with headroom for hundreds of enemies. Sim-tick M-items flatten p99; building batching and the architecture reserve (spatial grid, SoA, sim thread, Cull/Draw) stay gated behind soak evidence and are only pulled if the cheap stacks plus instancing miss the bar.

ranked_candidates.title: Disable vsync via loadPrcFileData('sync-video false') before Ursina() — frames are quantizing to vblank multiples at exactly the 30fps gate
ranked_candidates.mechanism: Panda default sync-video=True (measured on this box) and Kingdom never overrides it — verified ursina_app.py:64-71 constructs Ursina() with no vsync/prc config. Ursina 8.3.0's vsync kwarg is dead code: window._ready never consumes it, apply_settings re-sets vsync=True post-open (window.py:82), runtime disable is 'not yet implemented' (window.py:497-508). Soak evidence: out/fast/windowed dt clusters at 33.9-35.7ms (the 2-vblank bucket) against only ~23.5ms run_frame CPU — ~10ms/frame pure present-wait, pinning that combo at 28-29.5 fps just under the gate; maximized shows dt 43-48ms vs 30-33ms CPU with gpu_or_ursina spikes 24-39ms. Vsync also amplifies every micro-hitch (a slight vblank miss pays the whole next interval). [Merged: frame-loop:vsync-off-sync-video + panda-config:prc-vsync-off]
ranked_candidates.fix: In UrsinaApp.__init__ BEFORE Ursina() at game/graphics/ursina_app.py:64: loadPrcFileData('kingdom-perf','sync-video false'); re-assert it AFTER Ursina() returns (apply_settings writes sync-video True back into the prc store, which would re-enable vsync on any GL context rebuild e.g. fullscreen toggle). Env hatch KINGDOM_VSYNC=1 to restore. Optionally clock-mode limited + clock-frame-rate 60 to avoid uncapped spin in menus. Do NOT use the Ursina kwarg or window.vsync (both dead).
ranked_candidates.expected_gain: out/fast/windowed: 28-29.5 -> ~36-40 fps (work ~26-28ms no longer rounds to 33.3); out/fast/maximized: 21-23 -> ~25-30. Flips the borderline gate combo decisively over 30 and removes the 33<->50ms bucket alternation that reads as judder. Biggest gain-per-line in the entire list.
ranked_candidates.risk: Tearing (low: Win10 DWM composites windowed and borderless-maximized, preventing tears); Intel driver control panel could force vsync anyway — verify with the A/B soak before claiming the win; uncapped fps in light scenes (mitigate with clock-mode limited).

ranked_candidates.title: Replace HUD upload (tobytes RGBA + PNMImage band round-trip + setRamImageAs fallback) with native BGRA memoryview copy — verified byte-identical
ranked_candidates.mechanism: Every changed frame pays: pygame.image.tobytes(surf,'RGBA') full-surface swizzle at ursina_app_ui_overlay.py:97 (verified this session; measured 7.6-8.0ms at 1920x1032), full numpy row diff (:181-183, ~2.7ms), per-band temp PandaTexture->PNMImage->load_sub_image (:154-176, 7.2-10.7ms per 120-180-row band), and the >50%-dirty/>6-run fallback setRamImageAs(raw,'RGBA') (:207-210, measured 50.4-52.8ms — a guaranteed hitch; 1303 slowframes in the maximized soak, spikes 25-69ms). Hero-spawn dirties 3-4 scattered regions (top-bar count + message + radar dot + left panel) which trips exactly this fallback — it IS part of the spawn hitch. ranked_candidates.title: Patch Ursina Text font/resolution properties: per-Text FontPool.load_font reload + unbounded model-path growth is the spawn-hitch root cause (22x measured fix)
ranked_candidates.mechanism: Every unit spawn creates 1-2 Ursina Text labels (name at ursina_unit_sync.py:186/:276/:340/:396, gold at :192, tc at :444-451, all via ursina_unit_overlays.py:163-173 — creation site verified this session). Text.__init__ runs font_setter on EVERY construction (site-packages/ursina/text.py:52-65, :266-287): getModelPath().append_path(fonts dir) — a DUPLICATE dir per Text, model-path measured growing 16->216 dirs over ~200 Texts — then FontPool.load_font (4.1ms, 73% of cost) then font.clear() wiping the shared glyph cache. Measured: Text creation 5.25-6.04ms (one explorer) to 21ms (another, colder conditions), DEGRADING 5.9->7.3ms as the session accrues — a previously unknown time-degradation amplifier that directly threatens the min15-20 gate. A 6-enemy wave entering the zoomed-out viewport = ~35-40ms in ONE frame. Verified: attribute-level patching of Text.font_setter does NOT work (generate_properties_for_class bakes properties); class-property replacement does. [Merged: spawn-hitch:text-font-cache-patch (confirmed, root cause) + frame-loop:text-label-pool-spawn-hitch measurements]
ranked_candidates.fix: Startup monkeypatch next to the existing prewarm in game/graphics/ursina_app.py:289-301: replace utext.Text.font with property(orig.fget, cached_setter) using a {font_name: font_object} memo (only first load falls through — also stops the model-path append), and Text.resolution with a guarded setter that no-ops when abs(getPixelsPerUnit()-value)<eps (REQUIRED: sharing the font object otherwise hits Panda's get_num_pages()==0 assert — reproduced). Measured result with exactly this patch: Text creation median 0.27ms (22x), full hero-spawn render bundle (billboard+HP+name) 6.6ms -> 0.84ms (8x). Guard with try/except + stock fallback for ursina upgrades. One live screenshot to confirm labels pixel-identical.
ranked_candidates.expected_gain: Hero-hire spike shrinks ~5-11ms; 6-unit wave burst ~35-40ms -> ~4ms; kills the monotonic per-spawn slowdown and unbounded model-path growth taxing every later font/model lookup. Converts the visible spawn/wave micro-hitch (gate criterion #2) into sub-frame noise. Still required even under instancing-default (labels exist when zoomed in).
ranked_candidates.risk: Monkeypatching third-party properties — ursina upgrade could rename the setter (fallback guard). Shared-font semantics are UNCHANGED from stock (FontPool already shares; stock just kept clearing the glyph cache). Custom-resolution Texts would keep the default unless the guard falls back — none exist in game labels.

Each rebuild demotes the whole visible set + re-reveals every circle (world.py:237-249,166-205; 1.5-5ms each) PLUS discover_known_buildings_after_fog iterates every candidate building x every hero x footprint tile with no distance prereject (hero_profile.py:345-376; 148,362 _iter_building_tiles calls/300 ticks). A/B no-fog delta = 1.45ms/tick. Hero spawn adds a revealer -> forces a full rebuild + discovery scan on the spawn tick (part of the spawn hitch).
ranked_candidates.fix: (a) Throttle 3 -> 5-6 ticks (250-300ms fog latency at FAST, invisible zoomed out); (b) prereject per (hero,building): skip when squared grid distance > (r+building_diag)^2; (c) optionally incremental update_visibility (diff per-revealer grid positions already in the snapshot at fog.py:124) — needs a SEEN-demotion parity test.
ranked_candidates.expected_gain: (a)+(b) ~0.7-1.0 of the 1.45ms/tick + rebuild spikes 3.6-5 -> ~1.5-2ms; (c) another ~0.3ms/tick. Directly shrinks the hero-spawn tick spike.
ranked_candidates.risk: Fog reveal latency +~150ms (imperceptible); ranger tile-XP/discovery timing shifts a few ticks (digest moves — authorized); (c) needs the parity test.
evidence: game/sim/fog.py:38-42,117-128 (throttle + dirty snapshot); game/world.py:237-249,166-205 (demote-all + re-reveal-all)
game/sim/hero_profile.py:345-376 (no distance prereject)
Measured: A/B no-fog +1.45ms/tick; 1.5-5.0ms per rebuild; 148,362 _iter_building_tiles calls/300 ticks; 100 rebuilds/300 ticks

ranked_candidates.title: LAST RESORT: sim accumulator on a worker thread, render interpolates frozen double-buffered snapshots
ranked_candidates.mechanism: tick_simulation runs inline in the render frame (ursina_app_frame.py:93); at FAST, 2x ticks compound exactly when fps drops (ticks-per-frame rises toward the 4-cap). The render boundary is already thread-ready (frozen DTOs, snapshot.py:31-90 — the WK66-68 migration removed live-entity render reads) and the render thread spends most of its time in GIL-releasing C (pygame blits, memcpy, Panda renderFrame), so 50-80% of tick cost genuinely overlaps. Only worth XL effort if ranks 1-30 + S6 leave the gate unmet — rank 30 (dt scaling) captures most of the win for a fraction of the risk.
ranked_candidates.fix: SimThread owns the tick loop + builds snapshots post-tick into a double-buffered slot (atomic swap); input via the existing InputEvent queue; game_state dict frozen at handoff (audit live hero/building refs at sim_engine.py:482-486); LLM/event-bus stays sim-side; phase-gated behind KINGDOM_SIM_THREAD=1 with the single-thread path intact; digest/tests keep the headless single-thread path.
ranked_candidates.expected_gain: Removes ~6-12ms of tick from the render frame at FAST and ends catch-up compounding; on top of the full stack ~33 -> 40+ fps maximized.
ranked_candidates.risk: Highest-risk item in the list: torn reads from any remaining live-object UI access, AI callback thread-affinity, debugging cost. Explicitly LAST.
evidence: game/graphics/ursina_app_frame.py:93; game/engine_facades/lifecycle.py:141-166 (accumulator, 4-tick cap); game/sim/snapshot.py:31-90 (frozen boundary)
game/sim_engine.py:482-486 (live refs in game_state — the audit surface)

execution_order.rationale: Every later stack's keep/revert decision needs per-frame stage attribution inside min 15-20, an honest screenshot cadence (the current sync shots inject ~250ms hitches that would poison the gate numbers), the igLoop split (decides whether residual cost is GPU-draw vs run_frame CPU — which arbitrates the vsync/threading/HUD forks), and a quantified spawn-hitch metric. All flags dead-by-default; zero gameplay risk. The optional offscreen renderer bench can be added opportunistically if agents need headless iteration.
execution_order.gate: Harness self-check: soak CSV stage columns populated with real values; [fps-probe-window 900:1200] line emitted; one async screenshot pixel-matches the sync path; igloop_ms + outside_ms sane (sum to dt within ~1ms); [spawn-hitch] lines emitted on forced spawns; no fps delta with flags off.

execution_order.rationale: Three S-effort, zero-visual-cost engine knobs with the best gain-per-line in the list. Vsync alone likely flips out/fast/windowed over 30 (frames straddle the 33.3ms vblank boundary with ~10ms of pure wait). ignore=True + mouse.traverse_target=None remove pure Ursina-internal overhead that scales with entity count. Each independently env-revertible.
execution_order.gate: A/B 20-min soak at out/fast windowed AND maximized: [fps-probe-window] avg/p10 improve (windowed target: avg >30); screenshots show no tearing/artifacts; igLoop timer confirms the present-wait shrank. Revert via KINGDOM_VSYNC=1 / flag removal if worse.

execution_order.rationale: Directly attacks gate criterion #2 (hero-spawn hitch elimination) with the CONFIRMED root cause (font reload + model-path growth — also a session-long degradation amplifier threatening the min15-20 window) plus the uncovered C1-class prefab-swap leak that re-introduces time-degradation in construction-heavy real play. Prewarm + pool/budget mop up the remaining first-use and burst costs. All measured; orphan-free and font patch are the two highest-stakes correctness items in the whole plan.
execution_order.gate: Spawn-hitch capture: post_max/pre_p50 <= 1.5 over >=20 forced spawns in the gate window AND no frame >80ms attributable to spawn/construction; stage-swap regtest shows scene.entities flat after N swap cycles; one live screenshot confirms labels pixel-identical post font-patch.

execution_order.rationale: hudU is the single largest tracked CPU stage at maximized (11-14ms avg, 25-69ms spikes) and most of the windowed/maximized gap. The zero-copy rewrite is CONFIRMED byte-identical and supersedes (not reverts) the multi-band guardrail — update the guardrails doc in the same commit. Radar throttle makes the existing fingerprint early-out actually fire; watch-card throttle covers the real-play pinned path the soaks never exercised. Region split deliberately deferred to S8.
execution_order.gate: Soak CSV: hudU avg < 2.5ms at maximized with ZERO setRamImageAs-class hitch frames (>20ms hudU) in min 15-20; HUD screenshots pixel-identical vs before (incl. radar, messages, command bar, pinned watch card); igLoop timer shows no compensating GPU-DMA growth.

execution_order.rationale: Confirmed/cheap reductions of the 23ms legacy rend stage and frame-loop waste: ~6ms of measured no-op overlay writes, ~2-8.5ms of per-frame filesystem stats (grows with city size), the duplicate snapshot build, label LOD (pre-approved lever (a), double-duty as the instancing label-parity design), the fog-texture spike, and the churn/GC leftovers. These raise the legacy floor (which remains the rollback path), shrink spike sources, and are all measurable on the harness. Dropped here: overlay-batch-renderer (superseded by S6's instanced HP bars + label LOD) and half-rate HUD compose (superseded by S3).
execution_order.gate: Soak rend column drops >=5ms at spec on the legacy path; screenshots at both sides of the label-LOD threshold (plus selected-hero exception) reviewed; WK67 digest byte-identical for the behavior-neutral items (dirty gates, caches, snapshot dedup); fog texture output byte-identical (existing regtest).

execution_order.rationale: Sim runs inline at 2x tick rate at the gate speed; candidates cut mean tick ~5ms -> ~1.5-2.5ms and p99 from 10-35ms to <8ms (the sim-side micro-hitch class, incl. hero-spawn fog+A* ticks). Three items are CONFIRMED via A/B; the M items (fog cadence, AI cache, tree-blocking set) are independent and individually gated. fast-dt-scaling held LAST within the stack and taken only if the gate still needs sim headroom (digest re-baseline + balance soak required).
execution_order.gate: Headless tick bench at the gate scenario: mean <= 2.5ms, p99 <= 8ms over a 6000-tick soak; incremental-vs-full parity asserts green (tree dict, blocking set, fog visibility); WK67 digest byte-identical for the neutral items, re-baselined ONLY for the explicitly-authorized behavior shifts (A* quantization, cadence changes, dt scaling); soak tick column confirms on the GPU box.

execution_order.rationale: The only mapped route to >30 sustained at the maximized worst combo: measured 31.5/25.2 fps vs 13-15 legacy, blocked solely by overlay parity + 5 correctness gaps + the rejected motion model. Order inside the stack: HP bars -> parity gaps -> linear interp -> (label pool from S4's LOD design) -> flip with latch + '=0' hatch + stale-doc cleanup. Legacy path is KEPT as the rollback hatch (architecture's 'delete legacy' deferred). This is where the gate lands; with S1+S3 already in, expect ~35-40 windowed / ~30+ maximized. Prior-art compliance: the previous flip revert was root-caused (C7) and this flip is parity-gated, exactly as the WK123 plan prescribes.
execution_order.gate: On the GPU box: legacy-vs-instanced pixel diff at the deterministic 20-warrior scene with overlays MATCHING; screenshots at both zooms + hills (terrain-Y) + magic/heal volleys + guard click-pick; two_sided regression test green; then the full 20-min soak matrix with heroes maintained: min15-20 [fps-probe-window] avg >30 AND p10 >=27 at out/fast/windowed, avg >=28-30 maximized, spawn-hitch metric still passing. KINGDOM_URSINA_INSTANCING=0 rollback validated before merging.

execution_order.rationale: After units are instanced, buildings become the dominant remaining per-Entity/draw cost (and construction instantiation is the biggest remaining hitch class at 11-64ms). Template+flatten+copy_to addresses both with one design, on the never-built guardrails lever (b). Scheduled after S6 because the instancing soak tells us how much maximized headroom is still needed; skippable if the gate is already comfortably met (but recommended regardless for the hundreds-of-enemies/large-city direction).
execution_order.gate: Per-building-type AND per-construction-stage screenshot parity (broad coverage per memory rules — every prefab, damaged tint, construction tint); construction-event frame cost < 5ms in the spawn/hitch capture; maximized soak delta >= +1.5 fps or revert.

execution_order.rationale: Pulled only if S1-S7 leave the gate unmet at maximized, or when the owner actually scales to hundreds of enemies. Order within: hud-region-split (if igLoop still shows HUD DMA), threading experiment (cheap to learn, env-gated, measured in isolation), M_binary (if GPU-fill-bound), then the two scaling enablers (spatial grid, SoA) which are about the 300-enemy future rather than today's gate, and the sim worker thread strictly last (dt-scaling in S5 captures most of its win at a fraction of the risk).
execution_order.gate: Each item individually env-gated and A/B-soaked at the gate combo with variables isolated (never stacked with another experiment in the same run); keep only on a clean win: threading needs a crash-free 17-min soak + >=+2 fps; spatial-grid/SoA need brute-force-vs-optimized parity tests green + flat tick/pack cost at 300 spawned enemies; anything ambiguous reverts.
